issues.suggestion: Add tests for: (1) XML escaping of special characters in word labels, (2) signed-mode legend presence, (3) correct item-count cap at 30, (4) empty item list produces valid SVG. Tests can assert substring presence in the rendered string.
evidence: No RadialPlotTest found in src/test/
toSVG() computes spiral coordinates, scales by canvas size, conditionally renders a legend for 'signed' mode, and has XML escaping logic
The escapeXml() method handles & < > " ' — any regression would produce invalid SVG served to clients

issues.summary: Semantic-field endpoints use 'seed' (singular) and 'seeds' (plural) inconsistently for related operations
issues.suggestion: Standardize on 'seeds' (plural, comma-separated) for all three endpoints. For /api/semantic-field/explore, accept 'seeds' and use the first (or only) value as the single seed. Update Javadoc comments accordingly. This is a breaking API change — version or deprecate the old 'seed' parameter if clients depend on it.
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore: 'HttpApiUtils.requireParam(exchange, params, "seed")' — single seed parameter
handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti: 'HttpApiUtils.requireParam(exchange, params, "seeds")' — plural seeds parameter
handleSemanticFieldComparison: 'HttpApiUtils.requireParam(exchange, params, "seeds")' — plural seeds parameter
A client developing against these three related endpoints would encounter two different parameter names for the same conceptual input

issues.summary: 'nouns_per' parameter is available in single-seed explore but silently unavailable in multi-seed explore
issues.suggestion: Either support 'nouns_per' in multi-seed explore (as topCollocates for each seed), or explicitly reject it with a 400 error: 'if (params.containsKey("nouns_per")) { sendError(exchange, 400, "nouns_per is not supported in multi-seed mode"); return; }'.
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore: resolveExploreParams() parses 'nouns_per' with default 30
handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti: comment 'nouns_per intentionally not supported in multi-seed mode' — if a client sends nouns_per=50, it is silently ignored
Silent parameter ignore is worse than a documented 400 error: callers cannot tell their parameter was dropped

issues.summary: CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN is a hardcoded static constant with no runtime override
issues.suggestion: Read from system property: 'System.getProperty("cors.allow.origin", "http://localhost:3000")'. Pass to WordSketchApiServer constructor and down to HttpApiUtils methods, or use a static initializer for the property-based approach.
evidence: CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN = 'http://localhost:3000' — used in all response headers
Comment: 'Restrict to specific origins in production. Change this value ... when deploying'
No -D property, constructor parameter, or environment variable allows changing this

issues.summary: REST endpoints lack machine-readable or discoverable documentation for required parameters, types, and error contracts
issues.suggestion: Add a GET /api/schema or /api/help endpoint that returns a JSON listing of all endpoints with their required/optional parameters and response shapes. At minimum, extend the WordSketchApiServer Javadoc with a parameter table for each endpoint.
evidence: WordSketchApiServer Javadoc lists endpoints but not their parameters or error responses
Handler method Javadoc documents some parameters in inline comments but not in a discoverable format
No /api/schema, OpenAPI, or /api/help endpoint

issues.summary: /api/bcql accepts arbitrary-length BCQL patterns with no size limit or rate control
issues.suggestion: Add a simple per-IP or global rate limiter for /api/bcql. For a local single-user tool this is low priority, but if ever exposed beyond loopback, an exponential backoff or token-bucket limiter on the query executor would prevent accidental or intentional resource exhaustion.
evidence: SketchHandlers.handleBcqlQueryPost() reads request body: 'MAX_REQUEST_BODY_BYTES = 65536' constant is defined but — checking the code — body reading is size-limited to 65536 bytes, which is a reasonable cap
No rate limiting on the endpoint — a client can send many large BCQL queries in rapid succession
The endpoint executes arbitrary corpus-range queries against the BlackLab index, which can be expensive for certain pattern shapes

issues.summary: No mechanism to enable authentication when deploying beyond loopback — auth cannot be added without code changes
issues.suggestion: Document in the README that this server is for local use only and must not be exposed publicly. Optionally add a --allow-remote flag that requires also providing a --api-token; reject it without a token to make the security contract explicit.
evidence: Server binds to loopback by default but the binding address is hardcoded via InetAddress.getLoopbackAddress()
No -Dauth.token or -Dallow.remote system property to change trust model
A future --host 0.0.0.0 flag (if added) would expose all endpoints without any auth layer
